title: Zoned Numeric Packing Codes (O)
text: Zoned Numeric Packing Codes (O) A value with packing code O is a sequence of i bytes. The first byte contains the most significant digit. Every byte, with the exception of the last byte, is a digit encoded as an ASCII decimal digit character (0 through 9). The last byte encodes both the least significant digit and the sign: The ASCII characters for the decimal digits 0 through 9 represent an unsigned (positive) digit. The ASCII characters p through y represent -0 through -9 . For example, packing code O2 can represent the integer values -99 through 99 . Scaling No scaling can be specified for the O packing codes. Related Links Packing Codes for Numeric Data The Uniface Numeric and Float data types are associated with many packing codes. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
